rules.statement: Infrastructure components MUST provide mechanical coordination without strategic decision-making. Retained as normative architectural doctrine (CORE-Infrastructure-Definition.md §4.2), evaluated by governor/human architecture review, not by an automated mechanical check.
rules.rationale: This rule's mapping (component_responsibility, engine=knowledge_gate) was never implemented by any engine and dispatched to nothing since it was declared; it is removed rather than left declared-but-inert (#820 Group B). Whether a component makes a 'strategic decision' is a semantic judgment, not a pattern any CORE engine mechanically verifies, and no such check is invented here. CORE-Infrastructure-Definition.md §7.1 previously showed this rule's mapping annotated with a fabricated enforcement: blocking field that never existed in the actual mapping YAML (enforcement is a rule-document field, not a mapping field) and never reflected reality; that section is corrected. The principle remains binding: meeting it is the entry criterion for infrastructure status under §3/§6.2, verified by governor/human architecture review.

rules.statement: Infrastructure components MUST document their constitutional authority claims in module docstrings.

Infrastructure MUST remain domain-agnostic and contain no business logic. Retained as normative architectural doctrine (CORE-Infrastructure-Definition.md §4.2), evaluated by governor/human architecture review, not by an automated mechanical check.
rules.rationale: This rule's mapping (component_responsibility, engine=knowledge_gate) was never implemented by any engine and dispatched to nothing since it was declared; it is removed rather than left declared-but-inert (#820 Group B). Whether a module contains 'business logic' is a semantic judgment about domain coupling, not a pattern any CORE engine mechanically verifies, and no such check is invented here. The principle remains binding: infrastructure status under CORE-Infrastructure-Definition.md §3/§6.2 requires meeting this criterion, verified by governor/human architecture review at the point a component claims infrastructure status.

rules.statement: ServiceRegistry MUST NOT choose between service loading strategies based on semantic context.
Infrastructure MUST use explicit error handling and not suppress errors with bare except clauses.
